system_context.role: Python is ONLY a perception layer. It must not generate nodeIds, handle chunking, decide citations, or run RAG logic.
system_context.goal: Provide high-fidelity OCR, layout analysis, and math extraction to be consumed by Node.js for atomic node construction.

input_contract.description: What Node.js sends to the Python OCR Microservice.

payload.image_data: string (Base64 encoded PNG or JPEG of the page)

output_contract.description: What Python returns to Node.js. Python MUST NOT include nodeIds or chunking logic.

payload.reading_order_hints: array of region indices representing Surya's detected reading order

extracted_lines.text: string (Extracted text or LaTeX for math)

style.font_size: float (Derived from bbox height if native font size is unavailable)

mapping_requirements_for_node.description: How Node.js will process the Python output.
rules: Convert OCR lines/words into Atomic Nodes: Node.js will iterate through `extracted_lines` and `extracted_words` and instantiate `TextNode` or `MathNode` objects.
Assign nodeIds: Node.js will generate `node-${pageIndex}-${index}` sequentially based on the `reading_order_hints` provided by Python.

Preserve BBox Mapping: Python's absolute pixel bboxes (x0, y0, x1, y1) MUST be converted by Node.js into percentage-based bboxes (x, y, width, height) relative to page dimensions before inserting into the DocumentGraph.
Maintain Ordering: Node.js MUST sort the injected nodes according to Python's `reading_order_hints` to preserve multi-column or complex layout flow for the StructuredChunker.

Inject Styles for Chunker: Node.js MUST map Python's `style.font_size` and `style.is_bold` into the `TextNode.content.style` object. The `StructuredChunker` strictly relies on these to dynamically calculate adaptive line-heights and to isolate semantic Headings from Body text.
Handle Math vs Text: If `region_type` is 'Math', Node.js will wrap the text in LaTeX delimiters ($$ or \[ \]) and assign a specific style/type to ensure the StructuredChunker preserves it intact.

layout_rules.description: How Surya layout output maps to our system.
rules: Surya Output to Blocks: Surya's detected structural regions (paragraphs, headers) map directly to `extracted_regions`. Node.js uses these regions as hard boundaries; the StructuredChunker will not merge nodes across different regions.
Reading Order: Python MUST use Surya to determine the logical reading sequence (e.g., top-to-bottom, left-column then right-column) and return this as `reading_order_hints`.
Multi-Column Pages: Surya must identify separate columns as distinct regions. Python must order the `reading_order_hints` such that the entire left column is read before the right column.

fallback_rules.description: Rules for triggering OCR vs native extraction.
rules: Triggering OCR: Node.js will trigger the Python pipeline ONLY IF native extraction (e.g., pdf.js) yields an abnormally low text-to-image ratio, or if the user explicitly requests 'Deep OCR' mode.
Using Existing Text: If native extraction successfully retrieves text with valid bounding boxes and standard fonts, Node.js will bypass Python OCR entirely to save compute.

Detecting Image-Heavy Pages: Node.js will analyze the PDF page payload. If the page consists of a single large image spanning >90% of the viewport, it is flagged as scanned and routed directly to Python.

performance_constraints.description: Constraints for the Python Microservice running in Cloud Run.
rules: Environment: Deployed on Cloud Run (CPU-only or lightweight GPU if configured). Must boot quickly and handle cold starts.
Memory: Process within strict memory limits. Free large tensors (PaddleOCR/Surya models) from memory between batches if memory is constrained, or use persistent lightweight models.
Batching: Node.js will send requests batched per page. Python should process one page request at a time to prevent OOM errors, unless specifically configured for batched inference.
Response Time: Python must prioritize layout extraction and PaddleOCR text extraction over heavy Pix2Text math extraction unless math is explicitly detected or requested.
